published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference + schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid + acerenza_martinez_iriarte_sanchez_becerra_spini_2025_household_bounds: adjacent IV/interference or sharp-bound pieces, not dual-completion network-IV
auerbach_auerbach_tabord_meehan_2024_exposure_policy_relevance + nibbering_oosterveen_2025_iv_interference_policy: policy relevance and IV interference bounds, but no dual-exposure transport frontier
vazquez_bare_2023_iv_spillovers + imai_jiang_malani_2021_interference_noncompliance: IV spillover/complier effects, but not this dual-misspecification nontransport witness

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship-aspirant comparison cites Hoshino-Yanagi and Schroder et al. only at paper level, with no section/equation/page anchor for the claimed dual-completion gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The policy-frontier claim relies on Auerbach-Auerbach-Tabord-Meehan and Nibbering-Oosterveen but gives no location-level anchor to the precise policy-relevance gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The constructive object T_g(P) is advertised as exhibited, but Exhibits 9.1/9.2 use infeasible fractional take-up moments under deterministic principal-response tables and singleton exposure cells.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Generalization is only partial: the proposal adds IV/compliance structure, but the mandatory parent-corner reduction is algebraically wrong as written; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: §9 parent reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Under perfect compliance, T_i can still stratify cells, so kappa_{s,t} is Pr(S_i=s,T_i=t), not Pr(S_i=s); the parent ADE is not recovered without making T singleton or aggregating over t.

soundness_flags.label: Exhibits 9.1 and 9.2
soundness_flags.one_line: With singleton S,T, exactly-one encouragement, and deterministic h_i, cells like delta_{1,1,0,0}=1/2 are impossible: D_i(1,0,0) is fixed at 0 or 1.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The policy-relevant complier effect is not mathematically defined, and singleton tau_g(P) is not enough without an explicit aggregation/normalization formula.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: T_g(P) is defined as a feasible set of finitely many share vectors, but the statement calls it a finite polytope; projection of the enumerated union need not be a convex polytope.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff frontier appears to unfold the definition of transportability as invariance of the feasible share vector rather than giving an independent characterization.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair the parent reduction by either imposing T_i singleton/no instrumental exposure in the parent corner or redefining the reduction as an aggregation over t.

Replace Exhibits 9.1/9.2 with a feasible design, e.g. independent Bernoulli encouragement where Z_i=1 can co-occur with hidden-neighbor encouragement so deterministic h_i can generate fractional observed moments.

Define the policy-relevant complier effect as an explicit functional of cell effects and tau_g(P), including denominator/normalization, then restate the frontier as an independently computable condition rather than an iff over its own feasible-share set.
Add location-level anchors to the closest comparator claims, especially the Hoshino-Yanagi IV exposure mapping result and the Nibbering-Oosterveen policy-relevant IV bound.

recommended_next_step: Revise before Stage 0: fix the parent corner, replace the infeasible exhibits, and restate the transport frontier with an explicit policy-effect functional and anchored comparator gaps.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft widens the parent with IV noncompliance and a second exposure map, but the required parent-corner reduction and concrete witness are not valid as written.
